mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS levels over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid three-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that compress corporate profit margins, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to predict direction with statistical significance (p < 0.05) in backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts confirm broad-based consumer spending strength, which directly lifts corporate revenues and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces positive returns in fewer than 55% of occurrences, or the interaction term loses predictive power when either series is held constant in regression analysis.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in the index of industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and output, which translates into higher short-term equity valuations over the next 5 trading days during expansionary phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 5-day returns during expansionary phases, or the signal reverses direction when inflation expectations exceed 3%.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation print exceeding 0.4% triggers expectations of imminent monetary tightening, which raises discount rates and compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 60% of high-inflation trigger months, or the correlation between CPIAUCSL spikes and negative equity returns breaks down over a 24-month sample.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a decline in the unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, which supports wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields positive returns in fewer than 50% of cases, or the predictive alpha vanishes when controlling for monetary policy shifts in multivariate models.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product reflects expanding aggregate demand that directly increases corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth shows zero or negative correlation with 60-day forward returns over a 5-year rolling window, or Rank IC remains below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth falling below 0.2% month-over-month indicates supply-side stagnation that constrains economic output and corporate profitability, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in > 65% of low-growth trigger instances, or the signal fails to maintain directional accuracy when tested against a neutral benchmark.
